title: ERC4337 deterministic account factory using LibClone ERC1967 with inline initialize() call
trigger_patterns: owner encoded in upper bits of salt
explanation: The owner is encoded directly into the upper 160 bits of the salt — getting deterministic per-owner addresses for free without an extra factory parameter. After clone deployment, initialize(owner) is called with calldata laid out manually: the selector 0xc4d66de8 is written at offset 0x00 (right-aligned in the 32-byte word, occupying bytes 28-31 = offsets 0x1c-0x1f), and the owner address is written at offset 0x20. call(gas(), account, 0, 0x1c, 0x24, codesize(), 0) reads exactly 36 bytes (4-byte selector + 32-byte address) starting at 0x1c. Output is discarded by writing to codesize() with length 0. On revert, returndatacopy + revert at the free memory pointer bubbles the inner error. alreadyDeployed flag from LibClone makes createAccount idempotent — calling twice is a no-op rather than a revert.
when_to_apply: ERC4337 account factories using deterministic deployment (CREATE2) with one initial argument that fits in the salt
when_not_to_apply: Multi-argument initializers, factories needing non-deterministic addresses, accounts with constructor logic
